nodes.name: Zodiac
nodes.description: Western and Chinese zodiac signs and astrology

items.prompt: Which zodiac sign spans March 21 to April 19 and is symbolized by the Ram?
items.explanation:
First sign of the zodiac (Mar 21–Apr 19); marks the vernal equinox and the astrological new year
Fire sign, cardinal modality, ruled by Mars
Symbol is the Ram, from the Greek myth of the Golden Ram whose fleece Jason and the Argonauts sought
Constellation's brightest star is Hamal (Arabic for 'lamb'), about 66 light-years from Earth
Famous Aries: Leonardo da Vinci, Lady Gaga, Elton John, Emma Watson, Mariah Carey
One of the smaller zodiac constellations despite being first in the cycle

items.prompt: Which zodiac sign spans April 20 to May 20 and is symbolized by the Bull?
items.explanation:
Second sign of the zodiac (Apr 20–May 20)
Earth sign, fixed modality, ruled by Venus
Symbol is the Bull; in Greek myth, Zeus disguised himself as a white bull to abduct the princess Europa
Constellation contains the Pleiades (Seven Sisters) star cluster and the bright orange star Aldebaran
The Pleiades appear in the mythologies of cultures worldwide, from Greek to Maori to Japanese (Subaru)
Famous Taureans: Shakespeare, Queen Elizabeth II, Adele, Dwayne Johnson, Karl Marx

items.prompt: Which zodiac sign spans May 21 to June 20 and is symbolized by the Twins?
items.explanation:
Third sign of the zodiac (May 21–Jun 20)
Air sign, mutable modality, ruled by Mercury
Symbol is the Twins; represents Castor and Pollux (the Dioscuri) from Greek myth
Castor was mortal and Pollux immortal; when Castor died, Pollux asked Zeus to let them share immortality
Constellation's two brightest stars are named Castor and Pollux, representing the twins' heads
Famous Geminis: Marilyn Monroe, John F. Kennedy, Prince, Kanye West, Angelina Jolie

items.prompt: Which zodiac sign spans June 21 to July 22 and is symbolized by the Crab?
items.explanation:
Fourth sign of the zodiac (Jun 21–Jul 22); begins at the summer solstice
Water sign, cardinal modality, ruled by the Moon
Symbol is the Crab; in Greek myth, Hera sent a crab to distract Heracles during his fight with the Hydra
Heracles crushed the crab underfoot, but Hera placed it in the sky as a reward for its loyalty
Faintest of the zodiac constellations; contains the Beehive Cluster (Praesepe, M44)
Famous Cancers: Princess Diana, Meryl Streep, Frida Kahlo, Nelson Mandela, Tom Hanks

items.prompt: Which zodiac sign spans July 23 to August 22 and is symbolized by the Lion?
items.explanation:
Fifth sign of the zodiac (Jul 23–Aug 22)
Fire sign, fixed modality, ruled by the Sun
Symbol is the Lion; represents the Nemean lion slain by Heracles as the first of his twelve labors
Constellation's brightest star, Regulus ('little king'), marks the lion's heart; about 79 light-years from Earth
The only zodiac sign ruled by the Sun rather than a planet
Famous Leos: Barack Obama, Napoleon Bonaparte, Madonna, Coco Chanel, Alfred Hitchcock

items.prompt: Which zodiac sign spans August 23 to September 22 and is symbolized by the Maiden?
items.explanation:
Sixth sign of the zodiac (Aug 23–Sep 22)
Earth sign, mutable modality, ruled by Mercury (shares Mercury with Gemini)
Symbol is the Maiden; associated with Demeter, goddess of the harvest, or her daughter Persephone
Second-largest constellation in the sky (after Hydra); brightest star is Spica, representing a sheaf of grain
Contains the Virgo Cluster, a massive collection of over 1,300 galaxies about 54 million light-years away
Famous Virgos: Beyonce, Michael Jackson, Mother Teresa, Freddie Mercury, Keanu Reeves

items.prompt: Which zodiac sign spans September 23 to October 22 and is symbolized by the Scales?
items.explanation:
Seventh sign of the zodiac (Sep 23–Oct 22); begins at the autumnal equinox
Air sign, cardinal modality, ruled by Venus (shares Venus with Taurus)
Symbol is the Scales of Justice; the only zodiac sign represented by an inanimate object
Ancient Greeks originally considered these stars part of Scorpio's claws; Romans separated them into Libra
Associated with Astraea or Dike, the goddess of justice, who held the scales
Famous Libras: Mahatma Gandhi, John Lennon, Oscar Wilde, Kim Kardashian, Will Smith

items.prompt: Which zodiac sign spans October 23 to November 21 and is symbolized by the Scorpion?
items.explanation:
Eighth sign of the zodiac (Oct 23–Nov 21)
Water sign, fixed modality, ruled by Pluto (modern) and Mars (traditional, before Pluto's 1930 discovery)
Symbol is the Scorpion; in Greek myth, Gaia sent a scorpion to kill the boastful hunter Orion
Orion and Scorpius were placed on opposite sides of the sky so they would never appear together
Constellation's brightest star is Antares ('rival of Mars'), a red supergiant about 550 light-years away
Famous Scorpios: Leonardo DiCaprio, Marie Curie, Pablo Picasso, Bill Gates, Fyodor Dostoevsky

items.prompt: Which zodiac sign spans November 22 to December 21 and is symbolized by the Archer?
items.explanation:
Ninth sign of the zodiac (Nov 22–Dec 21)
Fire sign, mutable modality, ruled by Jupiter
Symbol is the Archer (centaur); represents Crotus, a satyr skilled in archery, placed among the stars by the Muses
Often confused with Chiron (represented by Centaurus), but Sagittarius derives from a different mythological figure
The center of the Milky Way galaxy lies in the direction of Sagittarius, near the 'Teapot' asterism
Famous Sagittarians: Winston Churchill, Mark Twain, Beethoven, Walt Disney, Taylor Swift

items.prompt: Which zodiac sign spans December 22 to January 19 and is symbolized by the Sea-Goat?
items.explanation:
Tenth sign of the zodiac (Dec 22–Jan 19); begins at the winter solstice
Earth sign, cardinal modality, ruled by Saturn
Symbol is the Sea-Goat (goat with a fish tail); from the myth of Pan, who leapt into the Nile to escape Typhon and was only half-transformed
One of the oldest recognized constellations, documented by Babylonians and Sumerians
The Tropic of Capricorn is named for this constellation (the Sun was in Capricorn at the December solstice in antiquity)
Famous Capricorns: Martin Luther King Jr., Muhammad Ali, Isaac Newton, Dolly Parton, Michelle Obama

items.prompt: Which zodiac sign spans January 20 to February 18 and is symbolized by the Water-Bearer?
items.explanation:
Eleventh sign of the zodiac (Jan 20–Feb 18)
Air sign (despite the water imagery), fixed modality, ruled by Uranus (modern) and Saturn (traditional)
Symbol is the Water-Bearer; represents Ganymede, a beautiful youth whom Zeus abducted to serve as cupbearer on Olympus
Despite the name and symbol, Aquarius is an air sign, not a water sign
The 'Age of Aquarius' refers to an astrological epoch popularized by the 1967 musical *Hair*
Famous Aquarians: Abraham Lincoln, Oprah Winfrey, Mozart, Charles Darwin, Rosa Parks

items.prompt: Which zodiac sign spans February 19 to March 20 and is symbolized by the Fish?
items.explanation:
Twelfth and final sign of the zodiac (Feb 19–Mar 20)
Water sign, mutable modality, ruled by Neptune (modern) and Jupiter (traditional)
Symbol is two Fish swimming in opposite directions, tied together; represents Aphrodite and Eros, who transformed into fish to escape Typhon
The vernal equinox point is slowly precessing into Pisces, giving rise to the concept of astrological 'ages'
Constellation is large but faint, with no stars brighter than fourth magnitude
Famous Pisces: Albert Einstein, Michelangelo, Rihanna, George Washington, Steve Jobs
